keywords.reason: powershell may indicate suspicious command-line or PowerShell behavior.
-enc may indicate suspicious command-line or PowerShell behavior.
-encodedcommand may indicate encoded PowerShell command execution.
iex is commonly used in malicious or obfuscated script execution.
Invoke-Expression is commonly used to execute PowerShell expressions or downloaded content.
DownloadString may indicate remote payload retrieval.
FromBase64String may indicate embedded encoded payload content.
WebClient may be used to download remote tools or payloads.
Start-Process may indicate child process execution.
cmd.exe may provide useful command-line execution context.
HTTP may indicate remote resource or payload access.
HTTPS may indicate remote resource or payload access.
Bypass may indicate attempts to evade execution policy or security controls.
Hidden execution may indicate an attempt to conceal command activity.
NoProfile usage may indicate an attempt to avoid loading normal PowerShell profile settings.
wscript can execute script content on Windows systems.
cscript can execute script content on Windows systems.
